title: fix(numericMenu): prevent refinement reset on checked radio click
body:
<!--
  Thanks for submitting a pull request!
  Please provide enough information so that others can review your pull request.
-->

**Summary**

<!--
  Explain the **motivation** for making this change.
  What existing problem does the pull request solve?
  Are there any linked issues?
-->


<!--
  Thanks for submitting a pull request!
  Please provide enough information so that others can review your pull request.
-->

**Summary**

<!--
  Explain the **motivation** for making this change.
  What existing problem does the pull request solve?
  Are there any linked issues?
-->

In the `NumericMenu` we bind an `onClick` event listener on the list item and change the radio button status at each click.

While this behavior is perfect for a list containing checkboxes (like a `RefinementList`) it does not fit for a list containing radio button, like the `NumericMenu` since it will reset the refinement if you click on an already selected radio button.

A way to fix this would be to use an `onChange` event listener on the radio button instead of the `onClick` on the list item (like the Angular and Vue implementations), but sadly in our case the radio button is made using a template and we cannot bind the event to it without affecting all widgets using `RefinementList`  :disappointed:.

The used solution was to prevent the refinement if the user click on an already checked radio button (so we do not affect `RefinementList` with checkboxes). This change is made at two places in the `RefinementList` component, depending if you directly clicked on the radio button or on one of it's parent.

A new unit test was added to test the new behavior, but I was not able to test a direct click on the input element (since it is created using a template we cannot simulate a click on it :cry:).

**Result**
